calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Hrg On Petition For Relief
Off Calendar Jul-15-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOSEPH S. MAY, TEL. NO. 781-3333 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER GARY LAWMAN'S Hearing On Petition For Relief from Government Claim is granted. Petitioner produced sufficient evidence to establish his condition of mental incapacity for the duration of the claim-filing period, and that his mental incapacity was the cause of his failure to file a claim. Petitioner's evidence established that Dr. Katz was qualified to render his opinion which was well supported by examination, interviews, physical examination and review of the medical records. CCSF misunderstands the requirements of Gov't Code sec. 946.6, which provides that petitioner must show failure to timely present the claim was due either to excusable neglect (see Gov't Code sec. 946.6(c)(1)) or to petitioner's mental incapacity (see Gov't Code sec. 946.6(c)(3)). Here, petitioner relies on the latter provision rendering CCSF's excusable neglect arguments irrelevant. Additionally, CCSF failed to produce any evidence to contradict petitioner's evidence. The court finds that the petition is supported by a preponderance of the evidence. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =(302/EHG)
